tax: Terrae Elementalis Minor is placed at species rank beneath Terrae (Lithic order, Earth Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a compact sub-1.8 m stone humanoid, lighter stone types, and faster movement than the standard form. It is separated from its sibling Terrae elementalis by its compact sub-1.8 m body and lighter stone versus the standard form's 2–3 m mixed-stone mass; the two share the genus body-plan but never the the larger mixed-stone standard mass. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: lighter stone types — sandstone, schist, and thin quartz — shaped by shallow fault pressure into a compact bipedal form.
ap: Terrae Elementalis Minor stands 1.2–1.8 m and 0.5–1.5 tonnes. Its silhouette is a compact stone humanoid with finer proportions, narrower plinth-feet, and a head-mass that shows some faceting. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: lighter stone types (sandstone, schist) visible in the body surface, contrasting with the darker mixed-stone standard form; faster, lighter movement — under half the floor-shake of the standard form at 10 m; and head-mass that shows slight faceting around the orbitals, giving it a more defined face than the standard's smooth oval. It moves with a quick, light stride that registers at 4–5 m on a tremor-rod rather than the standard's 10 m, and shows faint amber orbitals, dimmer than the standard form. Even after dispersal it can be identified from narrow plinth-impressions (20 cm wide vs 40 cm for standard) and lighter, shallower drag-groove, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Terrae Elementalis Minor persists in narrow side-passages, collapse galleries, and shallow fault seams of the upper-mid tiers where shallow pressure can hold a smaller mass. It does not breed; it forms where shallow fault pressure animates lighter stone into a compact humanoid body, and recurs whenever the shallow fault re-pressurises and the compact body re-forms from the same lighter stone. It sustains its mass on shallow fault pressure, drawing in lighter stone debris and schist flakes incorporated into the compact mass. Range is bounded by the shallow fault seam zone: outside that band the body loses coherence faster than the standard form; compact bodies break apart at greater distance from pressure. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a narrow side-passage where schist and sandstone fragments have moved upward in a concentrated line, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Terrae Elementalis Minor is roused by approach to within 5 m of its seam or the sound of tools in adjacent passages and otherwise lies a compact stone pile in a narrow passage, easily mistaken for a collapse. It claims a single narrow passage or side-gallery and answers intrusion with quick targeted blows and rapid retreat into the narrow passage where its smaller size is an advantage. Toward grey-folk it is more reactive and faster than the standard form; survey crews report that Crews in narrow side-passages now carry a short tremor-rod, since the minor form's 4–5 m detection range gives little warning. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Terrae Elementalis Minor: a body of lighter stone types — sandstone, schist, and thin quartz — shaped by shallow fault pressure into a compact bipedal form; immune to crushing but disperses faster under vibration than the standard form due to lighter stone; and a defining weakness — vibration and loss of shallow pressure, both of which disperse it rapidly. Its senses run through the medium itself: tremor sensing through plinth-base at 20 m, and shallow pressure sensing in its seam. It tolerates confined spaces, cold, and darkness but fails at vibration and loss of shallow fault pressure. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the compact 1.2–1.8 m body, lighter stone types, and narrow plinth-impressions, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Terrae Elementalis Minor are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it delivers quick targeted blows and retreats into the narrow passage; it uses the passage geometry to avoid retaliation from larger targets; it repairs from lighter stone debris faster than the standard form (smaller mass to fill); and it can squeeze through a 0.6 m gap, which the standard form cannot. Each is a direct expression of shallow fault pressure shaping a compact lighter-stone bipedal form, so it cannot, for instance, match the mass force of the standard or major form, or sustain itself far from its shallow seam. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Terrae Elementalis Minor matters because it patrols the narrow side-passages crews use as access routes and is faster and harder to detect than the standard form at short range. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a narrow-passage territorial hazard, requiring a short tremor-rod for detection, and its presence reshapes routes through the upper-mid side-passages, now tremor-tested before use as access routes. The Stonesong Heralds classify the minor form as the 'secondary voice' — the stone speaking in tight places where only a smaller body fits.
g: Terrae Elementalis Minor (Terrae elementalis minor) is the small-form earth elemental: a compact, quick Terrae body under 1.8 m, formed by shallow fault pressure and dispersed more easily than its standard kin. Documented from the narrow side-passages, collapse galleries, and shallow fault seams of the upper-mid tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a compact humanoid body of lighter stone — sandstone, schist, and thin quartz — held by shallow fault pressure — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Minor is not a juvenile of the standard form — it is a separate heritable body-plan formed at shallower pressure, with its own anatomy and field signs. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a scatter of smaller, lighter stone fragments — sandstone and schist — across the floor and no carcass.
